mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive returns occur in less than 55% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity repricing within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment is already above 7%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand recovery that sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce a positive 60-day return in at least 50% of observed cases over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in the industrial production index reflects immediate supply chain bottlenecks that compress short-term corporate output and force negative equity adjustments within one week.
failure_condition: Short-term equity returns remain positive in over 65% of severe industrial production drawdowns or the signal reverses direction during liquidity-driven market rallies.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation breaching the 2.5% annualized threshold triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening that raises discount rates and suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets post positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of high-inflation trigger months or the correlation between CPI growth and forward returns turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor force participation deterioration that undermines wage growth and generates negative equity returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases while UNRATE increases month-over-month
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in over 60% of cases or fails to separate from baseline market performance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP establishes a baseline trend in aggregate demand that directly translates into sustained corporate revenue expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth direction fails to predict 60-day equity direction with a Rank IC above 0.04 or positive returns occur in less than 50% of positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production expanding above 1.5% month-over-month signals robust manufacturing capacity utilization that supports immediate earnings revisions and drives positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in over 55% of strong industrial production months or the signal loses significance when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.
